ddls:
CREATE TABLE "isps" (
  "isp_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ISP */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the ISP */,
  "technology" TEXT /* Technology used by the ISP (e.g., Fiber, Cable, DSL, Satellite) */,
  "max_download_speed" REAL /* Maximum download speed offered by the ISP in Mbps */,
  "max_upload_speed" REAL /* Maximum upload speed offered by the ISP in Mbps */,
  "availability" REAL /* Availability of the ISP as a percentage */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the plan in USD per month */,
  "data_limit" INTEGER /* Data limit in GB per month */,
  "contract_length" INTEGER /* Length of the contract in months */,
  "installation_fee" REAL /* Installation fee in USD */,
  "customer_support" TEXT /* Availability of customer support (24/7, weekdays, etc.) */,
  "website" TEXT /* Official website of the ISP */,
  "logo_url" TEXT /* URL to the ISP's logo */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the ISP was added to the database */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the ISP's information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("isp_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_isps_technology FOREIGN KEY ("technology") REFERENCES technologies ("tech_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "technologies" (
  "tech_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each technology */,
  "tech_name" TEXT /* Name of the technology */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the technology */,
  "speed_range" TEXT /* Range of typical speeds for the technology (e.g., '100-1000 Mbps') */,
  "cost_effectiveness" INTEGER /* Cost-effectiveness rating from 1 to 5 */,
  "environmental_impact" INTEGER /* Environmental impact rating from 1 to 5 */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the technology was added to the database */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the technology's information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tech_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "isp_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ISP being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the review */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (1-5) */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the review was written */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text of the review */,
  "usefulness" INTEGER /* Number of users who found the review useful */,
  "speed_test_results" TEXT /* JSON object containing speed test results (download, upload, latency) */,
  "isp_plan" TEXT /* Specific plan the user is reviewing */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the review was written */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the review was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_isp_id FOREIGN KEY ("isp_id") REFERENCES isps ("isp_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the user registered */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile picture */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user is verified */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rankings" (
  "rank_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ranking */,
  "isp_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ISP being ranked */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the ISP (1, 2, 3, etc.) */,
  "rank_date" TEXT /* Date the ranking was calculated */,
  "total_reviews" INTEGER /* Total number of reviews for the ISP */,
  "average_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the ISP based on user reviews */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the ranking was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the ranking was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rank_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rankings_isp_id FOREIGN KEY ("isp_id") REFERENCES isps ("isp_id")
)
